method: Web search enrichment plus cross-check against MANIFEST GPS/altitude values. Altitude is marked official only when an external source gave a published value; otherwise the MANIFEST iPhone altitude is retained as project metadata.

IMG_7948_DONGAN.full_name: Dong'an, Daqing area, Heilongjiang Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.conclusion: Use MANIFEST coordinate. Daqing official/geographic sources cluster around 46.58-46.60 N, 125.00-125.10 E; 47.7443 N points far north of the Daqing/Qiqihar route and did not resolve to the project Dong'an context.
coordinates_verified.note: Treat 47.7443 as a one-degree latitude typo unless a later primary GPS source proves otherwise.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact Dong'an point.

IMG_7948_DONGAN.climate_note: Early July on the Songnen Plain is warm and humid compared with the later Inner Mongolia grassland segment.
IMG_7948_DONGAN.light_note: Evening light is low and long, with flat summer haze over a petroleum-city plain.
IMG_7948_DONGAN.one_fact: Daqing sits on the Songnen Plain and its city identity is tied to the Daqing Oil Field, discovered in 1959.
IMG_7948_DONGAN.narrative_note: The coordinate behaves like Daqing, not the first name written down. Oil country begins quietly, under ordinary summer light.

IMG_8033_DAMIN.full_name: Damin Town / Damin Subdistrict, Longsha District, Qiqihar, Heilongjiang Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: The coordinate sits just south-west of central Qiqihar, consistent with Damin and the airport-side urban edge.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for Damin; MANIFEST altitude is retained.

IMG_8033_DAMIN.climate_note: Midday in July is warm, relatively humid, and within Qiqihar's monsoon-influenced summer period.
IMG_8033_DAMIN.light_note: High summer noon light; broad sky and hard top-light softened by northeastern haze.
IMG_8033_DAMIN.one_fact: Qiqihar Sanjiazi Airport is listed as being located in Damin Town, about 13 km from the city center.
IMG_8033_DAMIN.narrative_note: Damin sits at the city edge, close enough to Qiqihar for signal and roads, far enough for the journey to start thinning out.

IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.full_name: Qiqihar to Arxan National Forest Park road corridor, Heilongjiang to Xing'an League, Inner Mongolia, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: This entry intentionally preserves the project's no-GPS state.

altitude_verified.note: The clip has no GPS altitude; Arxan scenic sources list high volcanic/lake terrain, including Tianchi at 1332.3 m, but that does not verify this road segment.

IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.climate_note: July is the green, wet, cool-season travel window in Arxan, with forest humidity and sudden mountain weather.
IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.light_note: Forest-edge light alternates between bright open grass and heavily absorbed conifer shade.
IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.one_fact: Arxan National Forest Park is known for volcanic landforms, primeval forest ecosystems, hot/mineral springs, wetlands, rivers, lakes, gorges, and peaks.
IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.narrative_note: The road goes toward Arxan and the phone stops knowing where it is. Green arrives before certainty does.
IMG_8084_QIQIHAR_ARXAN_ROAD_NO_GPS.signal_coverage_note: A 2026 regional telecom report says China Unicom improved continuous coverage at Arxan National Forest Park core viewpoints, visitor centers, and roads, which implies previous or off-core forest road coverage could be patchy. Project clip IMG_8084 remains no-GPS and is preserved as signal lost.

IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.full_name: Dalai Lake National Nature Reserve / Hulun Lake, Hulunbuir, Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST points cluster within roughly 0.2 km of the requested Dalai/Hulun Lake coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: IUCN/Ramsar material lists Hulun/Dalai Lake around 543 m; MANIFEST clip altitudes are slightly higher, plausible for shore/road level.

IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.climate_note: July is short, warm summer on a semi-arid continental lake plain; 2024 also carried regional wildfire-smoke risk from fires north-east of the border.
IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.light_note: Open steppe-lake light is wide and wind-cleared, but smoke can flatten contrast and turn distance into a veil.
IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.one_fact: UNESCO describes Dalai Lake as a biosphere reserve and a prime site for wetland and bird-migration research; Hulun Lake is the largest freshwater lake in northern China.
IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.wildfire_context: NASA Earth Observatory documented smoke streaming from intense fires around Amur Oblast near China's Heilongjiang border on July 9, 2024; Russia's Far East fire activity increased markedly in early July. This deepens but does not hour-match the Dalai/Hulun Lake clips from July 5, so the project should render this as regional smoke context rather than meteorological proof at the exact frame time.
IMG_8191_8220_8224_DALAI_LAKE_HULUN_LAKE.narrative_note: Hulun Lake is large enough to make distance look simple. Smoke can make the far shore disappear without moving it.

IMG_8284_YIRSHI.full_name: Yirshi / Yiershi Town, Arxan City, Xing'an League, Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official point altitude found for Yirshi; MANIFEST altitude is retained.

IMG_8284_YIRSHI.climate_note: July nights in the Greater Khingan foothills are cool relative to the plains, with forest moisture and rapid temperature drop after sunset.
IMG_8284_YIRSHI.light_note: Night light is town-scale and sparse; beyond settlement edges the image falls quickly into black.
IMG_8284_YIRSHI.one_fact: Yirshi is used as a route node for Arxan forest travel itineraries, connecting Arxan city, forest park roads, and surrounding wetland/mountain sites.
IMG_8284_YIRSHI.narrative_note: Yirshi is a settlement light at the forest edge. After it, the map becomes less confident.

IMG_8300_MOSITAI.full_name: Mositai / 莫斯台 area, Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: Coordinate matches request; external English-language information for 'Mositai' itself was sparse.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this named point.

IMG_8300_MOSITAI.climate_note: July afternoon is short-season grassland summer: warm sun, wind exposure, and a high chance of fast-changing cloud.
IMG_8300_MOSITAI.light_note: Open grassland produces high, unobstructed luminance with few built structures to interrupt horizon light.
IMG_8300_MOSITAI.one_fact: A gazetteer-style source glosses Mositai as a Mongolian-derived place name meaning “white stone,” but no strong official local page was found.
IMG_8300_MOSITAI.narrative_note: Mositai is barely a searchable place. The name stays small while the grass around it keeps going.

IMG_8722_JILIN_CITY.full_name: Jilin City, Jilin Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude for this exact point was found.

IMG_8722_JILIN_CITY.climate_note: July in Jilin City is warm, humid, and rainy under a monsoon-influenced continental climate.
IMG_8722_JILIN_CITY.light_note: Evening river-valley light is humid and reflective, with hills and the Songhua River softening the urban edge.
IMG_8722_JILIN_CITY.one_fact: Jilin City is built around the Songhua River and is surrounded by named mountains, a geography that gives the city its 'river city' identity.
IMG_8722_JILIN_CITY.narrative_note: Jilin folds river, city, and hills together. Evening makes the Songhua side of the city feel briefly held.

IMG_3484_BAIYIN_RENMINLU.full_name: Renminlu area, Baiyin City, Gansu Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: External weather/geographic sources list Baiyin around 1724 m, matching the MANIFEST altitude.

IMG_3484_BAIYIN_RENMINLU.climate_note: July in Baiyin is warm and semi-arid, with late-afternoon heat easing toward a dry plateau evening.
IMG_3484_BAIYIN_RENMINLU.light_note: Dust and high-elevation sun make the light hard, pale, and slightly chalked by the plateau air.
IMG_3484_BAIYIN_RENMINLU.one_fact: Baiyin's name literally means 'silver', and the city is strongly associated with mineral extraction and nonferrous metals.
IMG_3484_BAIYIN_RENMINLU.narrative_note: Baiyin begins the ascent with an urban street and a mining city name. The altitude is already there, before the mountain appears.

IMG_3549_3551_GULANG.full_name: Gulang County, Wuwei City, Gansu Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST point lies within the Gulang county area, north of the county seat coordinate listed by gazetteers.

altitude_verified.note: Published county elevation ranges run from about 1550 m to 3469 m; MANIFEST altitudes fit inside this range.

IMG_3549_3551_GULANG.climate_note: July is Gulang's warmest month but remains dry by eastern-China standards, with high evaporation and strong sun.
IMG_3549_3551_GULANG.light_note: Evening light is dry and granular, flattening into yellow-grey at the eastern edge of the Hexi Corridor.
IMG_3549_3551_GULANG.one_fact: Gulang County lies at the eastern end of the Hexi Corridor and south of the Tengger Desert.
IMG_3549_3551_GULANG.narrative_note: Gulang is the dry corridor before the high road. The evening light looks as if dust has been mixed into it.

IMG_3567_3612_3618_YANGXIANG_AREA.full_name: Yangxiang locality / 杨乡 area, Zhangye-Gansu to northern Qinghai highland corridor, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: Mapcarta resolves Yangxiang as a locality in Zhangye, Gansu at about 37.98977 N with elevation around 2605 m. MANIFEST clips form a nearby road corridor rather than a single town center.

altitude_verified.note: No official point altitude found; MANIFEST shows the road segment rising sharply into mountain terrain.

IMG_3567_3612_3618_YANGXIANG_AREA.climate_note: July is the brief green season on the Gansu-Qinghai highlands, with cool air at altitude and rapid cloud build-up.
IMG_3567_3612_3618_YANGXIANG_AREA.light_note: Light is high-UV and exposed; cloud shadows can move quickly across road, slope, and grass.
IMG_3567_3612_3618_YANGXIANG_AREA.one_fact: The Yangxiang sequence crosses from settled corridor country into a highland zone where the same day's clips jump from about 1953 m to above 3119 m.
IMG_3567_3612_3618_YANGXIANG_AREA.narrative_note: Yangxiang is not one exact place here, but a climb. The road keeps changing altitude faster than language can name it.

IMG_3773_3798_3810_QILIAN_MOUNTAIN_PASS.full_name: Qilian Mountains pass area near Qingshizui / Menyuan, Qinghai Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: Representative MANIFEST point is within roughly 1 km of the requested pass coordinate; IMG_3810 reaches the project maximum altitude.

altitude_verified.note: Published Qilian Mountains regional sources place much of the range around 3500-5000 m; MANIFEST values are plausible for a road/pass rather than a summit.

IMG_3773_3798_3810_QILIAN_MOUNTAIN_PASS.climate_note: July is the accessible alpine-meadow season, but the pass remains cool, wind-exposed, and sensitive to sudden weather.
IMG_3773_3798_3810_QILIAN_MOUNTAIN_PASS.light_note: Thin high-altitude air gives bright, hard edges; evening drops fast behind ridges and turns the route line into a pressure system.
IMG_3773_3798_3810_QILIAN_MOUNTAIN_PASS.one_fact: Britannica describes the Qilian Mountains' ice reservoir as a crucial water source for the Hexi Corridor and Qaidam Basin.
IMG_3773_3798_3810_QILIAN_MOUNTAIN_PASS.narrative_note: The highest point is not a summit, just a road becoming thin. The route line has already decided what the body must accept.

history_culture.protected_status: Qilian Mountain National Park pilot/protected-area system overlaps the broader Qilian ecological region, but the road clip itself is not a named protected site.
history_culture.local_admin_context: Near Qingshizui / Menyuan Hui Autonomous County, Haibei Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Qinghai Province.
history_culture.watershed_context: The Qilian Mountains feed the Hexi Corridor and Qaidam Basin through glaciers, snowmelt, alpine meadows, and mountain rivers.

IMG_3840_DONGXIA.full_name: Dongxia Town / 东峡镇 area, Datong Hui and Tu Autonomous County or Huangyuan-Datong eastern Xining mountain corridor, Qinghai Province

coordinates_verified.note: Search resolves multiple Dongxia/Dongxiaxiang references near Xining. The MANIFEST coordinate is kept as the clip truth; official Dongxia-town context is used for environment only.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact point.

IMG_3840_DONGXIA.climate_note: July at 3167 m is cool by season, with strong sun and colder air when cloud covers the plateau.
IMG_3840_DONGXIA.light_note: Midday light is intense and high-contrast, but plateau haze reduces distant saturation.
IMG_3840_DONGXIA.one_fact: Datong County sources describe Dongxia river/forest areas as part of the Beichuan/Huangshui watershed, with forested recreation areas around 2600-3300 m.
IMG_3840_DONGXIA.narrative_note: Dongxia holds the descent above three thousand meters. Nothing has returned to lowland height yet.

IMG_3940_GANDI_XINING_OUTSKIRTS.full_name: Gandi / Xining outskirts, Qinghai Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: Xining proper averages about 2200 m; this clip point is substantially higher than the city valley and is kept as MANIFEST altitude.

IMG_3940_GANDI_XINING_OUTSKIRTS.climate_note: Early July dawn near Xining's plateau edge is cool, dry, and marked by large day-night temperature swings.
IMG_3940_GANDI_XINING_OUTSKIRTS.light_note: Pre-sunrise plateau light is blue-grey and low, then brightens abruptly once the sun clears terrain.
IMG_3940_GANDI_XINING_OUTSKIRTS.one_fact: Xining lies in the Huangshui River Valley on the eastern edge of the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau and has been a northern Silk Road commercial hub for over 2000 years.
IMG_3940_GANDI_XINING_OUTSKIRTS.narrative_note: Near Xining, dawn does not feel like arrival. The plateau keeps its altitude under the road.

IMG_3682_DONGTAN.full_name: Dongtan / 东滩 area, Qilian-Menyuan highland corridor, Qinghai Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: Exact official locality was not strongly verified; MANIFEST coordinate places the clip in the northern Qinghai highland corridor.

IMG_3682_DONGTAN.terrain: mountain / plateau grassland / agricultural basin edge
IMG_3682_DONGTAN.climate_note: July evening is cool at 2635 m, with rapid loss of warmth after sunset.
IMG_3682_DONGTAN.light_note: The light drops behind ridges and leaves grass, road, and settlement edges in a blue-green aftertone.
IMG_3682_DONGTAN.one_fact: The clip sits between the lower Yangxiang segment and the later Qingshizui high pass, making it a middle-altitude hinge in the ascent route.
IMG_3682_DONGTAN.narrative_note: Dongtan is the route catching its breath. The altitude is already higher than a city, but not yet dramatic enough to announce itself.

IMG_3727_NANFENG.full_name: Nanfeng / 南丰 area, Qilian highland road corridor, Qinghai-Gansu border region, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: Search for 南丰 in Qinghai returns weak or conflicting results; coordinate remains primary.

IMG_3727_NANFENG.climate_note: July noon at about 2800 m is bright but not lowland-hot; wind and UV are the main weather facts.
IMG_3727_NANFENG.light_note: Light is overhead and unprotected, making the ground read flatter than its elevation.
IMG_3727_NANFENG.one_fact: This clip is the last mid-altitude point before the Qingshizui cluster climbs above 3400 m.
IMG_3727_NANFENG.narrative_note: Nanfeng is a name the map gives reluctantly. The road is more legible than the settlement.

IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.full_name: Lower East Side, Manhattan, New York City, specifically Ludlow Street between Stanton Street and Rivington Street

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST representative printed here is from IMG_1401 and sits east of the stated Ludlow coordinate; other project context fixes CH01 at 40.7194,-73.9896. Treat as a chapter location cluster, not one precise GPS point.

altitude_verified.note: LES is low-elevation urban terrain; no official altitude for the apartment point was searched beyond MANIFEST.

IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.climate_note: September evenings are humid and warm; late-night spring clips are cooler but still enclosed by building heat and street-level nightlife.
IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.light_note: The dominant light is artificial: bar spill, traffic, apartment windows, and the black reflective surface of high-rise glass.
IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.one_fact: NYC311 states that between 10 PM and 7 AM, music from bars/restaurants may not exceed 42 dB measured inside nearby residences and may not exceed 7 dB over ambient street level when measured from the public street.
IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.noise_context: Ludlow/Orchard/Rivington/Essex sit inside a documented nightlife and noise conflict zone. NYC311 has a specific complaint category for bar/club/restaurant noise, and local reporting describes Lower East Side late-night crowd/noise tensions around Ludlow and nearby streets.
IMG_1401_LES_CLUSTER.narrative_note: Ludlow between Stanton and Rivington does not sleep cleanly. The window is closed, then cracked, then still not enough.

IMG_0902_BENSONHURST.full_name: Bensonhurst, Brooklyn, New York City, New York, United States

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact point.

IMG_0902_BENSONHURST.climate_note: February midnight in Brooklyn is winter-cold, often damp, and street sound carries through hard residential surfaces.
IMG_0902_BENSONHURST.light_note: Light is residential sodium/LED streetlight with low winter reflectance and little sky color.
IMG_0902_BENSONHURST.one_fact: Bensonhurst is noted for major ethnic enclaves and is described as home to Brooklyn's second Chinatown.
IMG_0902_BENSONHURST.narrative_note: Bensonhurst is a winter edge of New York, residential and awake in small pieces. The city is quieter but not absent.

IMG_1113_JERSEY_CITY.full_name: Jersey City, Hudson County, New Jersey, United States

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact point.

IMG_1113_JERSEY_CITY.climate_note: February morning is cold and often windy across the Hudson-side urban plain.
IMG_1113_JERSEY_CITY.light_note: Winter morning light is hard and low, reflecting off glass towers, asphalt, and river haze.
IMG_1113_JERSEY_CITY.one_fact: Jersey City functions as part of the New York metropolitan waterfront rather than as a visual break from it; the river is a boundary but not a separation.
IMG_1113_JERSEY_CITY.narrative_note: Jersey City sits across the river like a room with the same weather. The skyline makes distance look administrative.

IMG_2719_COLD_SPRING.full_name: Village of Cold Spring, Putnam County, New York, United States

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact point.

IMG_2719_COLD_SPRING.climate_note: Mid-March in the Hudson Highlands is late winter/early spring, often cold with bare trees and bright river air.
IMG_2719_COLD_SPRING.light_note: Hudson Valley light is open over the water but broken by steep hills and leafless branches.
IMG_2719_COLD_SPRING.one_fact: Cold Spring's official village page ties its 19th-century growth to the West Point Foundry and its location on the Hudson River.
IMG_2719_COLD_SPRING.narrative_note: Cold Spring has the Hudson and the hill in the same breath. March keeps the trees undecided.

IMG_2685_FORT_MONTGOMERY.full_name: Fort Montgomery, Town of Highlands, Orange County, New York, United States

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: MANIFEST altitude is slightly below sea level, likely GPS noise near the Hudson; do not use this as literal elevation.

IMG_2685_FORT_MONTGOMERY.climate_note: March morning is cold and exposed near the Hudson, with winter vegetation still dominant.
IMG_2685_FORT_MONTGOMERY.light_note: Light opens sharply over the river and is interrupted by highland slopes and historic stonework.
IMG_2685_FORT_MONTGOMERY.one_fact: New York State Parks identifies Fort Montgomery as the site of a fierce Revolutionary War battle for control of the Hudson River on October 6, 1777.
IMG_2685_FORT_MONTGOMERY.narrative_note: Fort Montgomery is history beside the river, but the clip reads first as cold air and stone.

IMG_8669_PITTSBURGH.full_name: Pittsburgh, Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official altitude located for this exact point.

IMG_8669_PITTSBURGH.climate_note: Early March in Pittsburgh is late winter, with low sun, cold air, and frequent damp overcast conditions.
IMG_8669_PITTSBURGH.light_note: River-valley light is oblique and steel-grey, often softened by cloud and industrial haze.
IMG_8669_PITTSBURGH.one_fact: Pittsburgh sits at the confluence where the Allegheny and Monongahela rivers form the Ohio River.
IMG_8669_PITTSBURGH.narrative_note: Pittsburgh gathers at the rivers. The city folds downward into bridges before it opens again.

IMG_1043_LONG_ISLAND_CITY_COORDINATE_CHE.full_name: Long Island City, Queens, New York City, with MANIFEST coordinate check against Midtown Manhattan

coordinates_verified.note: The reverse-geocoder label says Long Island City, but the MANIFEST coordinate plots in Midtown Manhattan near the Madison Square Garden / Koreatown area, not across the East River in Queens. Treat the place name as uncertain.

IMG_1043_LONG_ISLAND_CITY_COORDINATE_CHE.climate_note: February afternoon in New York is winter-bright, often cold and clear between buildings.
IMG_1043_LONG_ISLAND_CITY_COORDINATE_CHE.light_note: If read as Long Island City, the light is East River skyline glare; if read as MANIFEST coordinate, it is Midtown street-canyon light.
IMG_1043_LONG_ISLAND_CITY_COORDINATE_CHE.one_fact: Gantry Plaza State Park in Long Island City preserves industrial gantries once used to load and unload railcar floats and barges on the East River waterfront.
IMG_1043_LONG_ISLAND_CITY_COORDINATE_CHE.narrative_note: The file says Long Island City, but the coordinate leans back into Manhattan. Even the label crosses the river incorrectly.

IMG_5523_ASCOT.full_name: Ascot, City of Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST representative point matches requested coordinate within rounding.

altitude_verified.note: Suburb sources list Ascot elevation around 14 m and Bartleys Hill at 78 m; MANIFEST points are low-lying local positions.

IMG_5523_ASCOT.climate_note: August is Brisbane's dry, mild late-winter period; May is autumn, still mild with humid residue at night.
IMG_5523_ASCOT.light_note: Low winter/autumn sun sits cleanly on roofs, river air, and pale road surfaces; night light is suburban and thin.
IMG_5523_ASCOT.one_fact: Ascot is strongly associated with Eagle Farm and Doomben racecourses; the suburb's name references Ascot, England.
IMG_5523_ASCOT.narrative_note: Ascot is close to water and still not soft. The commute begins before the day does.

IMG_6010_BALMORAL.full_name: Balmoral, City of Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST matches requested coordinate within rounding.

altitude_verified.note: No official exact altitude located; MANIFEST indicates a low river-adjacent point.

IMG_6010_BALMORAL.climate_note: May nights in Brisbane are mild autumn nights, often humid enough for air and water surfaces to hold glare.
IMG_6010_BALMORAL.light_note: Water reflections dominate: bright motion survives after the surrounding suburb goes dark.
IMG_6010_BALMORAL.one_fact: Balmoral is a compact inner-eastern Brisbane suburb bordering the Brisbane River and adjacent to Bulimba/Hawthorne.
IMG_6010_BALMORAL.narrative_note: Balmoral moves even at night. The river keeps reflecting after the rest of the suburb has gone flat.

IMG_3483_MORNINGSIDE.full_name: Morningside, City of Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST matches requested coordinate within rounding.

altitude_verified.note: No official exact altitude located for the clip point.

IMG_3483_MORNINGSIDE.climate_note: May morning is mild, with Brisbane autumn light after a short night and before midday heat builds.
IMG_3483_MORNINGSIDE.light_note: Morning light is already high enough to feel exposed; shadows are shorter than the emotional register expects.
IMG_3483_MORNINGSIDE.one_fact: Morningside developed with rail and light-industrial corridors, leaving visible layers of residential and industrial use.
IMG_3483_MORNINGSIDE.narrative_note: Morningside starts too early and too bright. Morning is not relief here.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_ROUTE_ASCOT_HAMILTON_TO.full_name: Brisbane CityCat F1 ferry route, Northshore Hamilton to The University of Queensland St Lucia

coordinates_verified.requested: Ascot/Hamilton to UQ St Lucia ferry commute
coordinates_verified.note: This is a route environment rather than a single clip GPS point.

altitude_verified.note: River route.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_ROUTE_ASCOT_HAMILTON_TO.climate_note: The commute occurs in Brisbane's subtropical climate: dry mild winter/August conditions and warm, humid shoulder-season conditions by May.
BRISBANE_CITYCAT_ROUTE_ASCOT_HAMILTON_TO.light_note: River light is persistent and reflective, especially in low sun; it can feel brighter than the air temperature suggests.
BRISBANE_CITYCAT_ROUTE_ASCOT_HAMILTON_TO.one_fact: Brisbane City Council describes CityCat/ferry services as stretching from UQ St Lucia to Northshore Hamilton, and the 2024 timetable shows Northshore Hamilton to UQ St Lucia all-stops trips of about 82-83 minutes.

ferry_data.vessel_type: High-speed, low-wash aluminium catamaran CityCat; Brisbane City Council fleet operated by RiverCity Ferries.
ferry_data.project_commute_note: User-reported 1.5 hours is consistent after walking/waiting/access time.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_ROUTE_ASCOT_HAMILTON_TO.narrative_note: The CityCat takes about eighty minutes from Hamilton to UQ. There is nothing to do but remain on the river.

IMG_0196_NANXIANG_SHANGHAI.full_name: Nanxiang Ancient Town, Jiading District, Shanghai Municipality, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: No official point altitude located.

IMG_0196_NANXIANG_SHANGHAI.climate_note: January in Shanghai is cool, damp, and low-sun, with evening air holding winter moisture.
IMG_0196_NANXIANG_SHANGHAI.light_note: Winter evening light is subdued and mixed with commercial/residential artificial light rather than bright skyline glare.
IMG_0196_NANXIANG_SHANGHAI.one_fact: Shanghai's official English site describes Nanxiang Ancient Town as over 1500 years old and named after Baihe Nanxiang Temple, built in 505 AD.
IMG_0196_NANXIANG_SHANGHAI.narrative_note: Nanxiang is before departure, but not innocent. The place is still there, which is not the same as return.

IMG_8863_TAIAN_MT_TAI_BASE.full_name: Tai'an / Mount Tai base area, Shandong Province, People's Republic of China

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST exactly matches requested coordinate.

altitude_verified.note: MANIFEST altitude fits a base/approach area; Mount Tai's Jade Emperor Peak is listed around 1532.7 m.

IMG_8863_TAIAN_MT_TAI_BASE.climate_note: Late December night at the base is cold; summit conditions are substantially colder because of the mountain's vertical climate gradient.
IMG_8863_TAIAN_MT_TAI_BASE.light_note: Night-climb light is artificial and directional: stair lamps, headlamps, vendor light, and black mountain mass.
IMG_8863_TAIAN_MT_TAI_BASE.one_fact: Mount Tai is a UNESCO World Heritage Site associated with imperial worship for thousands of years and is regarded as foremost among China's Five Great Mountains.
IMG_8863_TAIAN_MT_TAI_BASE.narrative_note: Mount Tai starts low enough to seem reasonable. At night the stairs remove that illusion one step at a time.

sparse_or_unverifiable.reason: User-provided latitude differs from MANIFEST by exactly one degree; external search supports the MANIFEST/Daqing-side latitude more than the requested coordinate.
English-language searchable sources for Mositai as a named place were sparse; terrain and signal notes are inferred from coordinate context and nearby regional sources.
Exact administrative identity and official altitude were not confidently verifiable; MANIFEST altitudes and Qilian/Gansu-Qinghai regional sources were used.
Exact local source for Dongxia at the clip coordinate was sparse; altitude is MANIFEST-only.
Only broad city context was gathered; no official altitude or neighborhood-specific environmental source was found.
Pittsburgh is not in the chapter structure; enrichment is enough for context but less deep than core project locations.
Summit/night-climb climate was contextualized from Mount Tai's altitude and mountain-climate sources; no December 2025 event-specific weather record was retrieved.

LES_LUDLOW_STANTON_RIVINGTON_BLOCK.full_name: Ludlow Street between Stanton Street and Rivington Street, Lower East Side, Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Project coordinate sits within the Lower East Side Ludlow/Stanton/Rivington nightlife cluster.

geography_environment.dominant_surface: asphalt, brick, glass, narrow sidewalks, high-density mixed-use blocks
geography_environment.climate: Humid summer nights and cold winter street canyons; building heat and bar traffic extend the day into early morning.

history_culture.local_admin_name: Lower East Side, Manhattan Community District 3
history_culture.historical_origin: Immigrant and tenement district that became a nightlife and music corridor through late 20th-century and 2000s redevelopment.

sensory_atmospheric.light: bar light, traffic reflection, apartment windows, sign glow
sensory_atmospheric.sound: crowds, amplified music, closing-time shouting, glass, delivery traffic
sensory_atmospheric.smell: beer, smoke, fried food, trash bags, wet pavement

access: NYC Subway nearby at Delancey St-Essex St
transport_infrastructure.notes: Noise conflicts are tied to the density of liquor licenses and late-night crowding rather than a single venue.

LES_LUDLOW_STANTON_RIVINGTON_BLOCK.one_fact: Community Board reporting cited 21 liquor licenses on a three-block stretch of Ludlow Street between Houston and Delancey, and local coverage describes Ludlow/Orchard/Rivington/Essex nightlife density as “Hell Square.”
LES_LUDLOW_STANTON_RIVINGTON_BLOCK.noise_context: NYC 311 has a dedicated bar/club/restaurant noise complaint category. Local reporting describes Ludlow between Stanton and Rivington as one of the Lower East Side blocks where residents expect late-night bar noise.
LES_LUDLOW_STANTON_RIVINGTON_BLOCK.narrative_note: Ludlow has too many places to be awake. The street keeps talking after the room has stopped answering.

STANTON_STREET.full_name: Stanton Street, Lower East Side, Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Referenced in eyu_articles.js snow essay; crosses Ludlow and sits within the CH01 neighborhood field.

STANTON_STREET.one_fact: The site of New York's second African burial ground is recorded between Stanton and Rivington Streets, now within Sara D. Roosevelt Park.
STANTON_STREET.narrative_note: Stanton appears first as a winter street. Bare branches, brick, and the phone call continuing anyway.

RIVINGTON_STREET.full_name: Rivington Street, Lower East Side, Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Rivington crosses Ludlow one block south of Stanton in the CH01 field.

RIVINGTON_STREET.one_fact: The intersection of Rivington and Ludlow was renamed Beastie Boys Square in 2023.
RIVINGTON_STREET.narrative_note: Rivington is one block down, close enough that sound does not need directions.

JIA_JIA_99_CENTS.full_name: JIA JIA 99¢ storefront, Lower East Side, Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Named in eyu_articles.js; exact address not verified in this pass.

JIA_JIA_99_CENTS.one_fact: The project text identifies the venue by its half-obscured sign, lottery ads, ATM light, and high-chroma yellow-green window glow rather than by an address.
JIA_JIA_99_CENTS.narrative_note: JIA JIA 99¢ is not found by address here. It is found by the green light and the cold getting worse.

MADISON_SQUARE_GARDEN_CLOCK_TOWER.full_name: Madison Square Garden / Penn Station area clock tower reference, Midtown Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Mentioned as a struck absent-photo text in eyu_articles.js; exact clock tower identity not verified.

MADISON_SQUARE_GARDEN_CLOCK_TOWER.one_fact: The reference is embedded in a paragraph about walking toward trains back to New Jersey, so its project function is transit memory more than landmark documentation.
MADISON_SQUARE_GARDEN_CLOCK_TOWER.narrative_note: The clock tower is crossed out. The route to New Jersey remains.

GENGHIS_KHAN_TETHERING_POST.full_name: Genghis Khan's Tethering Post / 成吉思汗拴马桩, west shore of Hulun Lake, New Barag Right Banner, Hulunbuir, Inner Mongolia

coordinates_verified.note: Search results place it on the west shore of Hulun Lake, 38 km northeast of A'zhen and 88 km from Manzhouli; exact authoritative coordinates were not found. Mapcarta lists it as a Hulunbuir scenic viewpoint.

altitude_verified.note: Use nearby Hulun Lake level context around 543-560 m, not a verified site altitude.

geography_environment.climate: Short warm continental summer; winter is severe and dry.

history_culture.local_admin_name: 新巴尔虎右旗 / New Barag Right Banner, Hulunbuir
history_culture.historical_origin: Tourism site based on the legend that Genghis Khan stationed and tethered horses here.
history_culture.protected_status: Part of broader Hulun Lake / Dalai Lake tourism and wetland context, not confirmed as an independent protected unit.

sensory_atmospheric.sound: wind, insects, tourist vehicles when present

transport_infrastructure.distance_from_arxan: not directly verified; site is on Hulun Lake west shore, closer to Manzhouli/New Barag Right Banner than Arxan city
transport_infrastructure.tourism_infrastructure: scenic viewpoint / tourism point, local guides and parking likely but exact facilities vary

GENGHIS_KHAN_TETHERING_POST.one_fact: Chinese encyclopedia entries describe the site as a three-sided-water cliff on the west shore of Hulun Lake, 38 km northeast of A'zhen and 88 km from Manzhouli, with a scenic area of about 17.5 square km including water surface.
GENGHIS_KHAN_TETHERING_POST.narrative_note: The tethering post stands by the lake because a story says a horse once had to stop there. The grass around it does not explain the story.

LANGSHAN_SCENIC_AREA_NANTONG.full_name: Langshan Scenic Area / 狼山风景区, Nantong, Jiangsu Province

coordinates_verified.note: Referenced in eyu_articles.js Langshan article; not tied to MANIFEST GPS.

LANGSHAN_SCENIC_AREA_NANTONG.terrain: low mountain / Yangtze River edge / urban scenic forest
LANGSHAN_SCENIC_AREA_NANTONG.population_density: high nearby / medium on mountain paths

geography_environment.dominant_vegetation: managed temple mountain vegetation, maple, ginkgo, evergreen plantings
geography_environment.climate: Late autumn is mild to cool in Nantong, with clear yellow light and river humidity.

history_culture.local_admin_name: 江苏省南通市崇川区狼山
history_culture.historical_origin: One of Nantong's Five Mountains and a long-standing Buddhist scenic site.
history_culture.protected_status: Nantong Five Mountains / Langshan scenic tourism area; broader Nantong records note Wushan area as national forest park context.

sensory_atmospheric.light: yellow late-autumn sun through maple and ginkgo
sensory_atmospheric.sound: steps, temple visitors, river wind, tour groups
sensory_atmospheric.smell: incense, leaves, river humidity, snack stalls
sensory_atmospheric.crowd_density: medium to high in good autumn weather

transport_infrastructure.access: Urban Nantong scenic area, reached by local roads and city transit; walking route climbs by steps through temple/scenic points.
transport_infrastructure.trail_distance: not confidently verified; visitor accounts describe short but slow stepped routes because of stops and viewpoints.

LANGSHAN_SCENIC_AREA_NANTONG.one_fact: Local tourism summaries list Langshan at about 106 m elevation, with Guangjiao Temple, Luobinwang Tomb, Zhiyun Pagoda, and other historic sites on the mountain.
LANGSHAN_SCENIC_AREA_NANTONG.narrative_note: Langshan is 106 meters. It still manages to make people pay attention to their breathing.

GUANGJIAO_TEMPLE_NANTONG.full_name: Guangjiao Temple / 广教寺 / 广教禅寺, Langshan, Nantong, Jiangsu Province

coordinates_verified.note: On Langshan; not tied to MANIFEST GPS.

altitude_verified.note: Temple is on/near Langshan summit area; precise temple-platform altitude not separately verified.

GUANGJIAO_TEMPLE_NANTONG.population_density: medium to high during temple/scenic visits

history_culture.local_admin_name: 南通市崇川区狼山广教寺
history_culture.protected_status: A nationally recognized key Buddhist temple in Han Chinese areas; Nantong yearbook records Guangjiao Temple as selected in 2013 into the seventh batch of national key cultural relics protection units as part of historic architecture.

GUANGJIAO_TEMPLE_NANTONG.one_fact: Guangjiao Temple is associated with Dashizhi Bodhisattva and is described as foremost among China's “Eight Lesser Buddhist Mountains.”
GUANGJIAO_TEMPLE_NANTONG.narrative_note: Guangjiao Temple began in 669. The steps are newer than that, but they still ask the body to agree.

HUAN_GONG_PAGODA.full_name: Huan Gong Pagoda / 幻公塔, Guangjiao Temple, Langshan, Nantong

coordinates_verified.note: Within Guangjiao Temple / Zilang Chanyuan area on Langshan.

history_culture.historical_origin: Built to commemorate Zhihuan, the Song-period monk associated with reviving Guangjiao Temple.

HUAN_GONG_PAGODA.one_fact: Nantong Buddhist sources describe Huan Gong Pagoda as a seven-storey, four-sided solid brick pagoda northwest of Zilang Chanyuan.
HUAN_GONG_PAGODA.narrative_note: Huan Gong Pagoda is the tower found first in the photograph. The tower itself stays less dramatic than the light around it.

BEILU_GARDEN_NANTONG.full_name: Beilu Garden / 北麓园, Langshan area, Nantong

coordinates_verified.note: Requested as a Langshan cultural site, but strong official English/Chinese web detail was not found in this pass.

BEILU_GARDEN_NANTONG.terrain: urban scenic garden / mountain foot context
BEILU_GARDEN_NANTONG.one_fact: The name indicates a garden/site at the northern foot of Langshan, but its official description remains unresolved after search.
BEILU_GARDEN_NANTONG.narrative_note: Beilu Garden is still mostly a name. It sits at the foot of the mountain and waits for a better source.

MOUNT_TAI_NIGHT_CLIMB_ROUTE.full_name: Mount Tai night climb, classic Red Gate - Zhongtianmen - Nantianmen - Tianjie - Jade Emperor Peak route, Tai'an, Shandong

coordinates_verified.note: MANIFEST point is base/Tai'an approach. Route climbs toward Nantianmen and Jade Emperor Peak.

MOUNT_TAI_NIGHT_CLIMB_ROUTE.terrain: mountain / stone stair route / urban base
MOUNT_TAI_NIGHT_CLIMB_ROUTE.signal_coverage: good on main route / weaker in mountain pockets

geography_environment.climate: December night conditions are cold, with possible ice and a summit substantially colder than Tai'an city.
geography_environment.vegetation: Lower mountain mixed forest and exposed stone stair sections near the upper route.

history_culture.protected_status: UNESCO World Heritage Site; one of China's Five Great Mountains.
history_culture.historical_origin: Imperial rites of fengshan sacrifice linked summit and base ritual geography.

sensory_atmospheric.light: headlamps, vendor bulbs, stair lamps, black mountain mass
sensory_atmospheric.sound: steps, breathing, vendor calls, wind near Nantianmen
sensory_atmospheric.smell: cold stone, instant noodles, sweat under rented coats

transport_infrastructure.distance: about 9-10.5 km depending source and start point
transport_infrastructure.stairs: about 6290 steps in common guide summaries
transport_infrastructure.walking_time: about 4-6 hours for night climb on foot

MOUNT_TAI_NIGHT_CLIMB_ROUTE.one_fact: The classic night route is the Red Gate line; guides describe Zhongtianmen at 847 m, Nantianmen at about 1460 m, and Jade Emperor Peak at 1532.7 m.
MOUNT_TAI_NIGHT_CLIMB_ROUTE.narrative_note: The base is 196 meters. The summit is 1532.7. At night, the difference is counted by knees.

NANTONG_SPORTS_PARK_HAO_RIVER.full_name: Nantong Sports Park / 体育公园 and Hao River / 濠河 scenic moat, Nantong, Jiangsu Province

coordinates_verified.note: Referenced in eyu_articles.js as a Nantong walking route via subway to Sports Park and around Hao River.

NANTONG_SPORTS_PARK_HAO_RIVER.population_density: high urban / medium along park walks

history_culture.founding_origin: Hao River moat dates to Later Zhou Xiande 5, 958 CE, according to Nantong history sources.
history_culture.protected_status: Hao River Scenic Area is a national 5A tourist attraction and one of China's best-preserved ancient moats.

transport_infrastructure.metro: Nantong Rail Transit Line 2 Sports Park Station opened on 2023-12-27 at Haoxi Road / Qingnian Middle Road, inside the Sports Park block.

NANTONG_SPORTS_PARK_HAO_RIVER.one_fact: Nantong history sources describe Hao River as about 10 km long, surrounding the old city in an inverted gourd shape and known as the city's “emerald necklace.”
NANTONG_SPORTS_PARK_HAO_RIVER.narrative_note: Sports Park has a subway station now. The old moat keeps making the walk look older than the city around it.

NANXIANG_ANCIENT_TOWN_DEEPENED.full_name: Nanxiang Ancient Town / 南翔古镇, Jiading District, Shanghai

history_culture.founding_origin: Shanghai official page describes Nanxiang as over 1500 years old and named after Baihe Nanxiang Temple, built in 505 CE.
history_culture.protected_status: Guyi Garden is a major classical garden site in Nanxiang.

NANXIANG_ANCIENT_TOWN_DEEPENED.one_fact: Nanxiang is associated with Guyi Garden and Nanxiang xiaolongbao as well as its older temple-town origin.
NANXIANG_ANCIENT_TOWN_DEEPENED.narrative_note: Nanxiang is before New York in the timeline. It is not departure, only proof that a place can remain without helping.

SCHOOL_OF_VISUAL_ARTS.full_name: School of Visual Arts / SVA NYC, Manhattan, New York City

coordinates_verified.note: Referenced by project documents and eyu_articles.js; not tied to one clip coordinate.

SCHOOL_OF_VISUAL_ARTS.one_fact: SVA was founded in 1947 as the Cartoonists and Illustrators School and later became the School of Visual Arts.
SCHOOL_OF_VISUAL_ARTS.narrative_note: SVA is not a backdrop here. It is the system that kept producing addresses, semesters, and reasons to stay.

UNIVERSITY_OF_QUEENSLAND_ST_LUCIA.full_name: The University of Queensland, St Lucia campus, Brisbane, Queensland, Australia

coordinates_verified.note: Connected to CH04 ferry commute; not a clip point.

UNIVERSITY_OF_QUEENSLAND_ST_LUCIA.one_fact: UQ describes St Lucia as a 114-hectare campus on Brisbane River-bound land, with sandstone buildings, gardens, lakes, and a CityCat ferry terminal.
UNIVERSITY_OF_QUEENSLAND_ST_LUCIA.narrative_note: UQ is across the river at the end of the ferry. The campus is green, which does not make the trip shorter.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_HAMILTON_TO_UQ_ST_LUCIA.full_name: F1 CityCat Northshore Hamilton - UQ St Lucia, Brisbane River, Queensland

coordinates_verified.note: Route connects Northshore Hamilton through inner Brisbane river wharves to UQ St Lucia.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_HAMILTON_TO_UQ_ST_LUCIA.population_density: high corridor / medium on vessel depending time

geography_environment.river_note: Tide tables for Brisbane Port are suitable for estimating river tide timing but not exact surface behavior at Balmoral/UQ.

transport_infrastructure.vessel_type: High-speed, low-wash aluminium catamaran CityCat; Brisbane City Council fleet operated by RiverCity Ferries.
transport_infrastructure.project_commute_note: User-reported 1.5 hours is consistent after walking/waiting/access time.

seasonal_light.ascot_august_2025: Brisbane August sunset is around 17:22-17:35 depending date; IMG_5523 at 17:20 sits at the edge of sunset light.

BRISBANE_CITYCAT_HAMILTON_TO_UQ_ST_LUCIA.one_fact: The official/all-stops F1 service runs daily between UQ St Lucia and Northshore Hamilton; one schedule example gives Northshore Hamilton 17:45 to UQ St Lucia 19:08, 83 minutes.
BRISBANE_CITYCAT_HAMILTON_TO_UQ_ST_LUCIA.narrative_note: Seventeen stops. Eighty-three minutes. The river does not care that this is a commute.

COPENHAGEN_KASTRUP_AIRPORT_TRANSIT.full_name: Copenhagen Airport, Kastrup / Københavns Lufthavn, Kastrup, Denmark

coordinates_verified.note: Referenced in eyu_articles.js as a Copenhagen transfer; not tied to MANIFEST GPS.

COPENHAGEN_KASTRUP_AIRPORT_TRANSIT.terrain: airport / coastal urban infrastructure on Amager
COPENHAGEN_KASTRUP_AIRPORT_TRANSIT.population_density: high in operating hours / sparse overnight pockets

transport_infrastructure.metro: M2 metro connects airport to central Copenhagen in about 14-15 minutes.
transport_infrastructure.rail: Mainline trains connect the airport with Copenhagen Central and regional/Øresund services.

COPENHAGEN_KASTRUP_AIRPORT_TRANSIT.one_fact: ICAO material describes Kastrup Airport as opening in 1925 and as one of the first airports designed exclusively for civil traffic.
COPENHAGEN_KASTRUP_AIRPORT_TRANSIT.narrative_note: Two hours in Copenhagen Airport. Enough time for the chairs to become a landscape.

BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.full_name: 北上 text route: Huainan - Zhengzhou - Anyang - Jinzhong - Fenyang - Linfen - Yuncheng - Weinan - Qingyang - Pingliang - Tianshui - Mianyang - Chengdu - Chongqing

coordinates_verified.note: No video/GPS from this route; the route exists as text in beishang.txt.

BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.terrain: plain / loess plateau / river valleys / Qinling-Daba mountain crossings / Sichuan Basin
BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.population_density: varies from dense cities to sparse plateau/rural corridors
BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.signal_coverage: good in cities / variable on mountain roads

geography_environment.landscape_sequence: Huai River/North China plain context into Yellow River basin cities, then Shanxi-Shaanxi loess country, Gansu corridor and Qinling-Daba transition, ending in the Chengdu-Chongqing basin.
geography_environment.watershed: Crosses or references the Huai, Yellow, Wei/Fen, and upper Yangtze cultural-geographic systems.

history_culture.route_type: personal retrospective route, not a named official itinerary
history_culture.cultural_context: Touches the north-south divide made by Qinling-Huaihe and the ancient Qin-Shu road logic connecting Guanzhong/Shaanxi and Sichuan.

BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.one_fact: Britannica describes the Qin Mountains as a sharp climatic and topographic divide between dry loess lands to the north and greener subtropical hills to the south.
BEISHANG_TEXT_ROUTE.narrative_note: 北上 names fourteen cities and then changes direction. The route is too long to become a memory cleanly.

NYC_SUBWAY_PATH_NJ_TRANSIT_CLUSTER.full_name: NYC Subway / PATH train / NJ Transit memory cluster between Lower Manhattan, New Jersey, and airport routes

coordinates_verified.note: Extracted from eyu_articles.js and project context; not one coordinate.

NYC_SUBWAY_PATH_NJ_TRANSIT_CLUSTER.one_fact: The project text uses transit as bodily memory: subway entrance, train back to New Jersey, airport transfer, and the 24-hour return route.
NYC_SUBWAY_PATH_NJ_TRANSIT_CLUSTER.narrative_note: The train is named after where it goes. The body remembers only the waiting area.

sparse_or_unverifiable_after_deep_search.reason: Official/detail source not found in this pass; retained as Langshan context but marked sparse.
Exact address could not be verified from search; kept as textual storefront reference.
Exact clock tower identity remains unresolved; kept as struck absent-photo reference.
Name found in gazetteer-style sources, but official local documentation remains sparse.
Coordinate retained from MANIFEST; official local identity remains unresolved.
